LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD RALSTON, FREDA KONG AND BANK OF AMERICA, N.A'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS COMPLAINT AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS FALL FAR SHORT OF MALICE, OPPRESSION OR FRAUD REQUIRED FOR AN AWARD OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFF IS ABLE TO ALLEGE ANY FACTS THAT WOULD SUPPORT AN AWARD OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

PROOF OF ELECTRONIC SERVICE OF CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100023874) FILED BY PLAINTIFF SMITH, ROSEMARIE G. AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RICHARD RALSTON, FREDA KONG AND BANK OF AMERICA, N.A'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AS TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT FOR ASSISTING FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE IS OVERRULED. WHILE THE STATUTORY LANGUAGE LACKS CLARITY AND THERE IS NO CASE LAW ON THE ISSUE, THE BETTER READING OF THE CURRENT VERSION OF WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE 15610.30(B) AND THE ONE THAT IS MORE PROTECTIVE OF THE RIGHTS OF ELDERS IS THAT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ASSISTING FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE CAN BE PREDICATED ON NEGLIGENT CONDUCT AND DOES NOT REQUIRE THAT THE "ASSISTER" HAVE ACTUAL KNOWLEDGE THAT ANOTHER PERSON OR ENTITY IS ENGAGING IN FINANCIAL ELDER ABUSE. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-25-2017 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Set For Trial On Jun-19-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2016 Continued To Nov-30-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jonathan A. Boynton of Majors & Fox LLP (619.234.1000) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Nicole N. King of Bryan Cave LLP (310.576.2270) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS RICHARD RALSTON, FREDA KONG and BANK OF AMERICA, N.A'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS COMPLAINT as to punitive damages allegations is GRANTED without leave to amend. Plaintiff's factual allegations fall far short of malice, oppression or fraud required for an award of punitive damages and it does not appear that plaintiff is able to allege any facts that would support an award of punitive damages. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jonathan A. Boynton of Majors & Fox LLP (619.234.1000) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Nicole N. King of Bryan Cave LLP (310.576.2270) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS RICHARD RALSTON, FREDA KONG AND BANK OF AMERICA, N.A'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as to the sole cause of action in the complaint for assisting financial elder abuse is OVERRULED. While the statutory language lacks clarity and there is no case law on the issue, the better reading of the current version of Welfare & Institutions Code 15610.30(b) and the one that is more protective of the rights of elders is that a cause of action for assisting financial elder abuse can be predicated on negligent conduct and does not require that the "assister" have actual knowledge that another person or entity is engaging in financial elder abuse. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
